report_ui.section1_title: I. Introduction: Career Survival Indicators for International Students in Korea
report_ui.section1_p1: The KVTI (Korea Visa Type Indicator) analysis model is an ultra-precise career diagnostic system designed based on the latest trends in the Korean labor market and psychological behavioral indicators. It analyzes key diagnostic indicators (industry fit, job disposition, organizational culture adaptability, etc.) from multiple angles to derive the applicant's unique disposition map, which is essential for international students to utilize their strengths in Korean society.
report_ui.section1_p2: This diagnostic report matches the persona that can best synergize with the applicant's potential. Based on this, **we find the optimal career path that perfectly matches the applicant's disposition and values, overcoming constraints within the broad categories of stay statuses officially permitted by the Korean Ministry of Justice (Professional Employment E-7, Tech Startup D-8, Excellent Talent Residency F-2, etc.)**. The analysis and recommended roadmap contained in this report will be the most realistic and proactive answer to how {{userName}} can achieve the highest job satisfaction by utilizing their own unique weapons in the unfamiliar environment of Korea.
report_ui.desc_R: Prefers practical operations, clear instructions, and tangible results.
report_ui.desc_I: Strong in data/source code analysis, logical reasoning, and problem-solving.
report_ui.desc_A: Pursues out-of-the-box UI/UX planning, design, and creative ideation.
report_ui.desc_S: Excels in client communication, team collaboration, and educational orientation.
report_ui.desc_E: Shows high interest in project leading, business, and negotiation.
report_ui.desc_C: Methodical, meticulous, and thrives in structured administrative and regulatory systems.
report_ui.style_a_desc: Solves problems meticulously based on data
report_ui.style_c_desc: Plans new ideas and creates original outputs
report_ui.style_p_desc: Handles practical affairs quickly by dealing with the field directly
report_ui.style_e_desc: Execution-driven style that persuades and leads projects
report_ui.section3_p1: By algorithmically matching the trait analysis model conducted earlier, we have derived the top 3 strategic target job clusters that best align with the respondent's traits and values. This guide is written based on realistic employment visa (E-7, etc.) acquisition models, but implies multidimensional career expandability such as starting a business (D-8) and long-term residency (F-series) depending on individual capabilities. The items below are examples of **official visa screening criteria of the Ministry of Justice** to enter the respective job clusters, and **practical core competency guidelines** to be planned in advance.
report_ui.special_guide: Special Entry Strategy Guide for International Students
report_ui.req_employer: Employer's National Employment Protection Screening Barrier:
report_ui.core_comp: [3] Strategic Core Competency for Visa Acquisition & Employment

report_ui.action_timeline: [4] Step-by-Step Visa Survival Integrated Roadmap (Actionable Timeline)
report_ui.red_flag: 🚨 Dept. of Justice Visa Revocation (Red Flag) Warning
report_ui.red_flag_desc: Even if a minor fine of less than 1 million KRW (jaywalking, illegal e-scooter riding, illegal part-time job, etc.) is paid during career preparation, it will act as a fatal deduction factor when switching to an F-2-7 resident visa in the future, fundamentally blocking long-term settlement in Korea. Strict compliance with laws and regulations is the absolute basic specification for career design.
report_ui.section4_title: VI. Conclusion & Future Tasks: Establishing a Proactive Career Roadmap
report_ui.section4_p1: The strategies and job recommendations presented in this diagnostic report are just the first blueprint for success. To achieve the final goals of employment and visa acquisition, building a focused career spec that perfectly fuses the prescribed legal requirements and your personal traits must be the top priority.
report_ui.section4_p2: To support {userName}'s successful settlement, we plan to refine and provide a **'Personalized Comprehensive Timeline Builder'** based on these diagnostic results in the future. Through this system, which will be provided on a continuous quest basis from the first semester of your freshman year, please complete your systematic life design in Korea.
report_ui.section5_title: Appendix: In-Depth Diagnosis Original Response Data
report_ui.section5_p1: This comprehensive diagnostic result was derived based on the survey data entered by the applicant below. The response to each question was utilized as a core variable for the E-7 visa suitability and optimal job matching algorithm.
report_ui.q_sec6: 6. Intent to Reside in Korea & Settlement Plan

questions.JP_R_01: I want to manage and inspect machinery in a factory or manufacturing site to ensure it doesn't stop.
questions.JP_R_02: I prefer assembling precise parts or meticulously inspecting products for defects.
questions.JP_R_03: I prefer working on-site, moving my body and sweating, rather than sitting in an office.
questions.JP_R_04: I want to understand the operating principles of cars or electronics and learn how to repair broken parts.
questions.JP_R_05: Operating machines accurately by reading blueprints or manuals suits my aptitude.
questions.JP_R_06: I want to install or finish structures at construction or interior design sites.
questions.JP_R_07: I would like to try growing crops and managing facilities in agriculture or smart farms.
questions.JP_R_08: I want an on-site job like driving a forklift or managing inventory in a logistics warehouse.
questions.JP_R_09: I enjoy making products by hand using wood, metal, leather, etc., with my dexterity.
questions.JP_R_10: I prefer an environment where I wear safety gear and strictly follow designated safety rules.
questions.JP_I_01: I want to collect and analyze complex data to find useful business results.
questions.JP_I_02: I want to develop new apps or websites using programming languages like Python or Java.
questions.JP_I_03: I want to conduct experiments to develop new technologies or advanced materials at a Korean research institute.
questions.JP_I_04: When a problem occurs, I want to find a solution based on logical reasoning and numbers rather than intuition.
questions.JP_I_05: Handling complex mathematical calculations or statistical programs is interesting to me.
questions.JP_I_06: I want to find software bugs and modify code to optimize systems.
questions.JP_I_07: I want to thoroughly research market reports and trend indicators when formulating marketing strategies.
questions.JP_I_08: I enjoy learning new knowledge and find reading professional academic papers or technical documents enjoyable.
questions.JP_I_09: I prefer handling tasks through objective facts and cause analysis rather than appealing to emotions.
questions.JP_I_10: I want to work as a research assistant in fields requiring professional knowledge, such as medicine or biotechnology.
questions.JP_I_11: I want to design concrete algorithms or system structures rather than abstract ideas.
questions.JP_A_01: I want to create advertising images to promote Korean products using my own creative ideas.
questions.JP_A_02: I want to design website or mobile app interfaces (UI/UX) to be user-friendly and aesthetically pleasing.
questions.JP_A_03: I want to plan and edit video content to be uploaded on platforms like YouTube, Instagram, or TikTok.
questions.JP_A_04: I want to translate or adapt Korean webtoons, dramas, or novels to fit the sentiments of my native language.
questions.JP_A_05: I want to work expressing my individuality in a free environment rather than following strict rules.
questions.JP_A_06: I want to propose new styles in trend-sensitive fields like fashion, beauty, or interior design.
questions.JP_A_07: I want to do marketing that moves people's hearts by writing articles or copy.
questions.JP_A_08: I want to plan and direct cultural and arts events such as stage performances, exhibitions, or festivals.
questions.JP_A_09: I consider aesthetic aspects like the color or shape of a product to be more important than just implementing features.
questions.JP_A_10: I enjoy proposing new methods or original solutions that others haven't tried.
questions.JP_S_01: I want to be a guide who introduces and guides Korean culture to foreign tourists visiting Korea.
questions.JP_S_02: I want to interpret and help foreign patients receive seamless medical care at hospitals.
questions.JP_S_03: I find it rewarding to teach my native language or major knowledge to others and help them grow.
questions.JP_S_04: I can excel in service roles addressing customer complaints and solving their problems.
questions.JP_S_05: I prefer collaborating and communicating with team members to achieve results together rather than working alone.
questions.JP_S_06: I want to do social work counseling and supporting international students or immigrants struggling with life in Korea.
questions.JP_S_07: I want to welcome guests at a hotel or resort front desk and provide top-notch service.
questions.JP_S_08: Meeting people, talking with them, and empathizing with their emotions suits my aptitude.
questions.JP_S_09: In sales, I prioritize building human trust relationships over logical persuasion.
questions.JP_S_10: I want to work at a duty-free shop or department store recommending and selling products that suit customers.
questions.JP_E_01: I want to introduce Korean company products to overseas buyers, negotiate prices, and win contracts.
questions.JP_E_02: I want to lead projects helping Korean companies expand into overseas markets utilizing my language skills.
questions.JP_E_03: I enjoy giving presentations in front of many people and persuading them with my opinions.
questions.JP_E_04: I want to be a team leader, setting goals and managing team members by giving task directions.
questions.JP_E_05: I want to take on roles where I receive incentives by achieving high sales targets in a competitive environment.
questions.JP_E_06: I want to conceptualize new business items and start my own business or run a startup in Korea.
questions.JP_E_07: I want to plan marketing campaigns and execute budgets to increase brand awareness.
questions.JP_E_08: I want to get promoted quickly within a company and become an executive making important decisions.
questions.JP_E_09: I tend to actively find and propose tasks rather than passively waiting for instructions.
questions.JP_E_10: I enjoy exchanging business cards with strangers at business meetings or networking parties.
questions.JP_E_11: I have the ability to persuade or influence others to lead them in the direction I want.
questions.JP_C_01: I want to handle and manage export/import documents (invoices, packing lists) accurately without typos.
questions.JP_C_02: I prefer accounting/bookkeeping tasks, recording company income and expenses, and organizing receipts.
questions.JP_C_03: I want to work in a stable environment with fixed working hours and clear task manuals.
questions.JP_C_04: I am good at organizing massive amounts of data in Excel to create visually appealing tables or graphs.
questions.JP_C_05: I find it more comfortable to strictly follow set rules and procedures rather than coming up with creative ideas.
questions.JP_C_06: I want to check product inventory quantities and input shipping/receiving details into computer systems.
questions.JP_C_07: I want to record meeting minutes meticulously and systematically classify and store documents.
questions.JP_C_08: I want to review laws or regulations to ensure there are no issues in the company's contracts.
questions.JP_C_09: I process even repetitive tasks steadfastly and have the meticulousness to avoid mistakes.
questions.JP_C_10: I prefer dealing with tasks according to planned schedules and prefer predictable tasks over uncertain situations.
questions.CMP_K_01: I am capable of listening to and understanding everyday and business Korean conversations.
questions.CMP_K_02: I can compose business emails or write formal sentences in Korean.
questions.CMP_K_03: I can naturally handle business phone calls coming in Korean.

questions.CMP_K_09: I understand the technical terms and practical vocabulary in my desired job field in Korean.
questions.CMP_K_10: I feel little fatigue even if I continuously communicate or attend a meeting in Korean for a long time (over 1 hour).

questions.CMP_C_05: Even if it differs from my opinion, I can positively accept work instructions from my superior.
questions.CMP_C_06: I can quickly grasp the atmosphere of the organization and demonstrate 'Nunchi' (social perceptiveness) to cope with it.
questions.CMP_C_07: I undoubtedly follow the company's internal rules and information security guidelines without exception.
questions.CMP_C_08: I am proficient at processing given tasks quickly and accurately before the deadline.
questions.CMP_C_09: I am ready to commit myself more to the performance of the team and organization, rather than personal achievement.
questions.CMP_C_10: I'm willing to accept negative feedback about my work from superiors or colleagues and improve upon it.
questions.CMP_G_01: I can write business documents utilizing various functions in Microsoft Excel.
questions.CMP_G_02: I can design well-arranged planning documents with high readability using PowerPoint (PPT).
questions.CMP_G_03: I can flawlessly write documents natively formatted in Hancom Office (HWP), widely used in Korean public institutions.
questions.CMP_G_04: I can proficiently use the latest in-house collaboration messengers and tools such as Slack or MS Teams.
questions.CMP_G_05: I can quickly and accurately collect basic data and statistical information from the internet required for work.
questions.CMP_G_06: I cleanly and systematically manage work files on desktops or cloud storage.
questions.CMP_G_07: I am familiar with basic manners like the format and greetings required when sending a business email.
questions.CMP_T_01: I can completely translate or interpret mutually between my native language (or English) and Korean.
questions.CMP_T_02: I can naturally converse and exchange emails in English safely in business practice.
questions.CMP_T_03: I possess overall essential knowledge related to trade practices, such as importing and exporting.
questions.CMP_T_04: I can effectively discover new buyers or new customers natively or overseas.
questions.CMP_T_05: I have the knowledge required to draft professional documents such as trade contracts or sales invoices.
questions.CMP_T_06: I have an active mindset capable of approaching new people first and proposing business sales.
questions.CMP_T_07: I possess the negotiation skills needed to successfully persuade business partners to maximize the company's profit.
questions.CMP_T_08: I have an overall understanding of basic marketing modeling and measuring demand prediction.
questions.CMP_I_01: I can directly write and execute code utilizing at least one programming language.
questions.CMP_I_02: I can directly architect and construct web services, either backend or frontend.
questions.CMP_I_03: I can handle and optimize databases (DBs) capable of managing large scales of user information.
questions.CMP_I_04: I can collaborate with colleagues and manage source codes using version control tools like Git and GitHub.
questions.CMP_I_05: I can logically identify and solve system errors or complex technical problems within programming.
questions.CMP_I_06: I comprehend server and infrastructure environments allowing applications to run stably.
questions.CMP_I_07: I can collect and process massive loads of data to unearth meaningful analysis models from it.
questions.CMP_I_08: I can acquire and apply newly introduced programming technologies or tools faster than anyone else.
questions.CMP_D_01: I can utilize professional graphic tools like Photoshop and Illustrator at a working-level competency.
questions.CMP_D_02: I am capable of seamlessly editing video contents utilizing Premiere Pro, After Effects, etc.
questions.CMP_D_03: I hold a design portfolio or personal performance outcomes able to prove my capabilities.
questions.CMP_D_04: My sensory grasp of visual trends ranging from the latest pop-up stores to fashion and interior design is extremely high.
questions.CMP_D_05: I possess profound comprehension of UI/UX, planning how users would comfortably feel in app/web environments.
questions.CMP_D_06: I retain the communication skills precisely deriving abstract planning drafts into a designer's language or actualized visual outputs.
questions.CMP_D_07: I can conduct 3D modeling frameworks or various structural drafting tasks using diagrams (CAD, etc.) and computer gears.
questions.CMP_V_01: I perfectly check and govern my immigration provisions and my sojourn status's epoch dates.
questions.CMP_V_02: I can smoothly function under South Korea's administrative systems, from HiKorea to HomeTax and Minwon24.
questions.CMP_V_03: I clearly identify the precise visa prerequisites (ex: E-7 specifics) crucial for post-graduation employment in Korea.
questions.CMP_V_04: I sustain fundamental taxation awareness necessary for a Korean worker such as income tax rates, the four major social insurances, and year-end evaluations.
questions.CMP_V_05: I apprehend cautionary protocols and real estate laws necessary alongside leasing properties (Jeonse/Monthly pay) transparently, whether striking a direct agreement or going through a certified realtor.
questions.CMP_V_06: I explicitly know my labor rights bound to the Labor Standards Act when securing part-time or full-time employment.
questions.CMP_V_07: As an international scholar, I am strictly aware of job-seeking regulations mandatory prior to securing a career or internal internships inside South Korean enterprises.
questions.CMP_WAGE_01: Considering my past practical experience and work capabilities, I possess the core competency to confidently demand an entry-level salary aligned above the average rates of fresh graduates universally in Korea (GNI 80% or greater).

questions.PER_R_01: I do not find it burdensome to have meals or drinks with company colleagues outside working hours.
questions.PER_R_02: I tend to be polite and take care of table settings when superiors or seniors are present.
questions.PER_R_03: Even if it's not a work-related conversation, I try to talk to colleagues first and get close.
questions.PER_R_04: I prefer eating lunch together with team members on a unified menu rather than eating alone.
questions.PER_R_05: When a superior gives work instructions, I positively accept it first even if it differs from my thoughts.
questions.PER_R_06: I try to read unspoken intentions (Nunchi) to grasp the atmosphere of the organization.
questions.PER_R_07: I politely use honorifics to people older or higher in rank than me.
questions.PER_R_08: When the company's decision conflicts with my personal interests, I can prioritize the company.
questions.PER_R_09: I can confidently hand out my business card at meetings with unfamiliar Korean people without feeling intimidated.
questions.PER_R_10: If a conflict arises within the team, I try to mediate even if I take a slight loss.
questions.PER_R_11: Even if I receive questions outside of work (age, marital status, etc.), I can understand it as Korean culture and brush it off.
questions.PER_R_12: It is important to attend family events (weddings, funerals) of colleagues after work or on weekends.
questions.PER_C_01: I believe deadlines must be met no matter what happens.
questions.PER_C_02: I continually report the intermediate progress to my superior even if the task is not completed.
questions.PER_C_03: If given an urgent task to be handled 'bali-bali' (quickly), I can speed up and respond.
questions.PER_C_04: I understand that overtime or holiday work may be necessary during important project periods.
questions.PER_C_05: I have a habit of arriving at least 10 minutes before start time to prepare for work.
questions.PER_C_06: If a mistake occurs during work, I try to admit and fix it immediately rather than making excuses.
questions.PER_C_07: My work desk is always organized, and I strictly follow file management rules.
questions.PER_C_08: Even if the company's security regulations or internal rules are somewhat strict, I thoroughly comply with them.
questions.PER_C_09: Even if given multiple tasks at once, I prioritize and handle them systematically.
questions.PER_C_10: I have a habit of taking notes, so I don't miss and record verbal instructions from my superior.
questions.PER_C_11: I refrain from using personal SNS or messenger apps during working hours and focus on work.
questions.PER_C_12: I feel comfortable only after I complete my assigned tasks to the end without passing them on to others.
questions.PER_M_01: Even if I get scolded by a superior for work matters, I shake it off without taking it emotionally.
questions.PER_M_02: I have my own ways to overcome loneliness or homesickness during life in Korea.
questions.PER_M_03: Even if I get stressed due to complex visa issues or administrative procedures, I solve them without giving up.
questions.PER_M_04: I handle sudden increases in workload or schedule changes without panicking.
questions.PER_M_05: Even if I make mistakes due to a lack of Korean skills, I am not discouraged and try again.
questions.PER_M_06: I do not feel that colleagues treat me specially or discriminate against me because I am a foreigner.
questions.PER_M_07: Rather than being anxious about an uncertain future (employment, visa), I focus on what I need to do now.
questions.PER_M_08: Even in an angry situation, I can calmly resolve it through conversation without expressing emotions.
questions.PER_M_09: When experiencing failure, I look for the cause within myself rather than blaming others or the environment.
questions.PER_M_10: I can maintain my own pace even within the fierce competitive atmosphere of Korean society.
questions.PER_M_11: I mostly enjoy the stress coming from adapting to a new environment.
questions.PER_M_12: I have a friend or mentor in Korea whom I can confide in when I go through hard times.
questions.PER_O_01: Even if Korean corporate culture (K-Culture) differs from my home country's, I accept it interestingly.
questions.PER_O_02: I regularly look up and study the latest news, trends, and slang in Korea.
questions.PER_O_03: I try to understand the behavioral and thinking styles of Korean people.
questions.PER_O_04: I enjoy learning new software or Korean-style work tools if they help my work.
questions.PER_O_05: I prefer an environment working together with colleagues of various nationalities.
questions.PER_O_06: I respect event cultures such as traditional Korean holidays or company anniversary days.
questions.PER_O_07: When my stereotypes are proven wrong, I flexibly change my mind and accept it.
questions.PER_O_08: I consider the Korean 'Jeong (Affection)' culture or human bonds important.
questions.PER_O_09: If there is a more efficient method than the existing one, I want to propose and change it.
questions.PER_O_10: I have no aversion to trying Korean food when choosing a meal menu or dinner gathering spot.
questions.PER_O_11: I usually try my best to observe Korean society's etiquette (greeting manners, public etiquette).
questions.PER_O_12: If there's something I don't know, I actively ask, 'I am sorry, but could you please explain again?'.
questions.PER_E_01: I want to pioneer a new market or run a personal business (startup) with my own skills or items.
questions.PER_I_01: Rather than being tied to a specific company, I want to work freely as a freelancer based on my skills (development, design, translation, etc.).
questions.VAL_01: I have never told a lie since I was born.
questions.VAL_02: I always like everyone and have never been angry.
questions.VAL_03: I am reading every question of this test meticulously.
questions.VAL_04: I have never missed an appointment time.
questions.VAL_05: Please select 'Strongly Agree' for this item.
questions.VAL_06: I have never made a mistake in my life.
questions.VAL_07: I have never spoken ill of others.
questions.VAL_08: Please select 'Strongly Disagree' for this item.
questions.VAL_09: I perform everything I know 100% perfectly.
questions.VAL_10: I never get nervous in any situation.
questions.VAL_11: Even if I'm in a bad mood, I never show it.
questions.VAL_12: I perfectly obey all the laws of the world.
questions.LIF_VIS_01: I have a firm desire to make Korea my 'Second Home'.
questions.LIF_VIS_02: If the visa problem is resolved, I plan to reside in Korea for the long term (over 5 years).
questions.LIF_VIS_03: I actively make Korean friends and enjoy blending into their culture.
questions.LIF_VIS_04: Life in Korea is safe, convenient, and overall satisfying.
questions.LIF_VIS_05: Even if it's financially difficult right now, I can invest my present for future career stability.
questions.LIF_VIS_06: I positively consider getting married or starting a family in Korea in the future.
questions.LIF_VIS_07: My goal is not simply to make money but to become an acknowledged expert in Korean society.
questions.LIF_VIS_08: Korea's fast work pace and fierce competitive environment actually stimulate me.
questions.LIF_VIS_09: Rather than planning to return to my home country, I prioritize settling in Korea.
questions.LIF_VIS_10: I am aiming for acquiring permanent residency (F-5) or even citizenship in Korea.
questions.LIF_VIS_11: Even if I don't go to grad school right now, I have the intention to get a Master's/Ph.D. in Korea in the long term.

result.footer_text:
This result is AI-analyzed data based on the Ministry of Justice E-7 review standards and Immigration Act.
Actual status change reviews may involve various additional variables (criminal records, tax arrears, etc.).
result.error_desc: Could not load result data. Please try again.
result.default_char: A talent with balanced organizational adaptability and job expertise
result.sum_p1_text: Our analysis shows that {{name}} resonates most deeply with the {{persona}} inclination. Notably, your intrinsic certainty (Career Clarity) was calculated at {{cvi}} points. Deconstructing detailed Work Style stats, the '{{topStyleName}}' inclination stood out most at {{topStyleVal}}%, followed and supported by the '{{secondStyleName}}' inclination ({{secondStyleVal}}%). Your macroscopic industry synergy driven by data is focused on a hybrid of {{topIndName}} ({{topIndVal}}%) and {{secondIndName}} ({{secondIndVal}}%) traits. Based on this, you can exert the highest internal certainty and immersion when designing a roadmap for the {{jobs}} fields.
result.sum_p2_text_high: Excellent major match and clear goal orientation
result.sum_p2_text_low: Flexible job adaptability exploring various possibilities
result.sum_p2_text_common: (Derived {{score}}% based on the job cluster). If you materialize this matching degree through future certifications or internship portfolios, it will serve as a great stepping stone to entering the E-7 visa employment market.
result.sum_p3_flexible: Analyzing your system and organizational interaction preferences, you showed a significant receptiveness of {{val}}% for 'Horizontal/Startup' centric environments. Researching structurally innovative global venture startups or active corporate cultures rather than strict hierarchies provides a much more advantageous direction.
result.sum_p3_hierarchical: Analyzing your organizational culture interactions, you showed strong receptiveness of {{val}}% towards stable and systematic environments centered on 'Vertical/Corporate' cultures. We recommend exploring mid-sized to large corporate cultures where systematic processes and reporting structures are well established.
result.major_exception_desc: Bachelor's+ and 1 yr practical exp OR replaceable via KOTRA Excellence Recommendation
result.red_flag_desc: Serious risks detected that must be checked before visa application.
result.extra_desc: Highlight {{name}}'s unique talents to offset any major mismatch.
